name: org.machanism.machai:assembly-maven-plugin
description: assembly-maven-plugin is a Maven plugin (packaging: maven-plugin) in the Machanism MachAI ecosystem that automates project "assembly" by combining GenAI-assisted library recommendation with an automated application assembly workflow driven by bindex metadata. It provides the Maven goal "assembly" (requiresProject=false, requiresDependencyCollection=NONE) so it can be executed outside a standard Maven project and applies changes to a configured base directory (${basedir}). Workflow: (1) obtains a free-form assembly prompt either from a configured prompt file (default project.txt, property assembly.prompt.file) or interactively via Plexus Prompter; (2) loads configuration from bindex.properties using org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator (Configurator abstraction); (3) runs org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker using a configurable GenAI provider/model (property org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker.MODEL_PROP_NAME, default org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker.DEFAULT_MODEL) and optional bindex registration/lookup URL (property bindex.register.url) to recommend relevant libraries as org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex entries, filtered by a minimum score threshold (property assembly.score, default org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly.DEFAULT_SCORE_VALUE), and logs recommended library ids with their scores (via picker.getScore); (4) runs org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly using a configurable GenAI provider/model (property org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly.MODEL_PROP_NAME, default org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly.DEFAULT_MODEL) to apply changes to the project directory, setting the project dir to ${basedir} and optionally logging inputs controlled by org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly.LOG_INPUTS_PROP_NAME. Primary use cases: generating/modifying a project structure and dependencies based on a natural-language prompt; recommending libraries based on semantic search over bindex metadata; automating integration/assembly steps in a Maven-managed directory. Key dependencies include MachAI bindex-core (1.0.2), Maven plugin APIs/annotations, Plexus Interactivity (Prompter), SLF4J, and test tooling (JUnit Jupiter, Mockito).

constructors.description: Factory method used by the Mojo to create a Picker instance configured with the picker model (pickModel) and optional bindex registration URL (registerUrl), using the provided Configurator as a configuration source. Intended primarily for testability/overriding picker creation.

examples.description: Creates a Picker using a PropertiesConfigurator-backed configuration source.

constructors.description: Factory method used by the Mojo to create an ApplicationAssembly instance configured with the assembly model (assemblyModel), configuration source (Configurator), and Maven execution base directory (basedir). Intended primarily for testability/overriding assembly creation.

examples.description: Creates an ApplicationAssembly and sets the project directory before running the assembly workflow.

features.name: execute
features.description: Executes the Maven goal "assembly". Reads the assembly prompt from a configured file (default project.txt, property assembly.prompt.file) if it exists; otherwise prompts the user interactively via Plexus Prompter. Creates a Configurator backed by bindex.properties. Runs MachAI Picker (configured by pickModel and optional bindex.register.url), applies a minimum score threshold (assembly.score, default ApplicationAssembly.DEFAULT_SCORE_VALUE), logs the recommended org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex ids and their picker scores, then runs MachAI ApplicationAssembly (configured by assemblyModel) against the Maven base directory (${basedir}). Optionally enables input logging based on ApplicationAssembly.LOG_INPUTS_PROP_NAME. Wraps I/O and prompting failures in MojoExecutionException.

examples.description: Illustrates programmatic execution of the Mojo (typically Maven invokes this automatically during a build or direct goal execution).

examples.name: Maven goal usage (snapshot)
examples.description: Run the assembly workflow against a directory using a prompt file (default project.txt) or interactively if the file is missing.

examples.name: Prompt source behavior
examples.description: If the prompt file does not exist, the plugin asks for a free-form prompt via Plexus Prompter and then proceeds with picker recommendations and application assembly.
